calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-04-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Allen Byerly, T:415.988.2111, defendant and moving party, appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ALLEN BYERLY's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT, as follows:

Defendant's motion to vacate the default entered on 4/25/19 and the default judgment entered on 5/2/19 is granted. Defendant demonstrates that he was not properly served with the summons and complaint and that the default/default judgment are void. (See Code of Civ. Proc., 473(d).) The service of the summons and complaint is quashed.

Plaintiff's proof of service for the summons and complaint states that plaintiff personally served a slight 65-year old bald gentleman at 1359 Valencia St., Apt. 1. Defendant's declaration establishes that defendant never resided at that residence and has no physical resemblance to the served sexagenarian. Defendant is a 40-year-old man who weighs 285 lbs. and possesses a full head of hair. That record conclusively rebuts any presumption of proper service. The court observes that plaintiff's brief appears to relate to an entirely different case, as it discusses the service of a woman and argues that "she" provides no proof that she was on an airline flight. (Opp., pg. 2.) Defendant is a man and his declaration has nothing to do with an airline flight.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

REQUEST PENDING TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Allen Byerly (415-988-2111) Defendant in propria persona.
No other appearances.

Discussed, not in compliance with tentative ruling rule. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLEN BYERLY's Motion To Vacate Default Judgment is Off calendar. Defendant Allen E. Byerly did not serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). Defendant may refile and re-serve the motion for hearing on proper notice.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
